id: hunting lateral movement (mitre ta0008)/remote desktop protocol (rdp) (t1021 001)
title: Remote Desktop Protocol (RDP) (T1021 001)
relPath: Hunting Lateral Movement (MITRE TA0008)/Remote Desktop Protocol (RDP) (T1021 001).md

breadcrumbs.id: hunting lateral movement (mitre ta0008)/remote desktop protocol (rdp) (t1021 001)
breadcrumbs.title: Remote Desktop Protocol (RDP) (T1021 001)

overview: RDP is a Microsoft protocol built into Windows for remote administration, enabling a user to access and control another system via a graphical interface. It’s widely enabled in enterprise environments, making its traffic generally considered legitimate.

study.overview: RDP is a Microsoft protocol built into Windows for remote administration, enabling a user to access and control another system via a graphical interface. It’s widely enabled in enterprise environments, making its traffic generally considered legitimate.
study.whySoc: Use this note as an analyst reference: understand the concept, identify the relevant telemetry, pivot through evidence, and document the decision path clearly.
workflow: Identify the user, host, process, network flow, or identity object involved.
Collect the highest-value logs first and preserve timestamps and assumptions.
Pivot through related notes, telemetry sources, and MITRE references.
Document findings, gaps, containment options, and detection improvements.

splunkExamples.code:
index=windows (EventCode=4624 OR EventCode=4688 OR EventCode=4778 OR EventCode=4779)
| eval RDP_Process=if(match(ProcessName,"mstsc.exe|rdpclip.exe|tstheme.exe"),"RDP Process","Other")
| stats values(ProcessName) as Processes by Computer, User, EventCode, LogonType, IPAddress
| where LogonType=10 OR RDP_Process="RDP Process"
